decisions.decision: Transactional outbox pattern for all event publishing
decisions.rationale: Decouples GL/audit/account-setup handlers from business transactions. GL failures no longer roll back business operations. Eventually consistent (1-5s) is acceptable.

decisions.decision: Accept eventual consistency for GL posting
decisions.rationale: Small delay between business commit and GL entry is acceptable for a single small SACCO (<500 members). Outbox processor runs every 5 seconds.

decisions.decision: Full outbox for all 18 event types with dead letter + retry
decisions.rationale: Unified reliability guarantee. Failed events move to dead letter table. Exponential backoff retry (5min, 15min, 1h, 4h). Max 5 retries before FAILED status.

decisions.decision: Outbox infrastructure in sacco-common, processor in sacco-app
decisions.rationale: All modules already depend on sacco-common. Outbox entity, repository, writer go there. The @Scheduled processor lives in sacco-app.
Single file to review all transition rules. More flexible than enum methods. Discoverable. Covers LoanStatus, PayoutStatus, ContributionStatus, MemberStatus.
Fail-fast behavior. Consistent enforcement. No risk of caller forgetting to check a boolean return value.

decisions.decision: Explicit correlationId field on AuditableEvent interface
decisions.rationale: All 18 event records get a correlationId field. Enables tracing business operations across downstream events. More visible than MDC-only approach.

decisions.decision: Idempotency key on outbox entries for de-duplication
decisions.rationale: Each outbox entry has a unique key (event_type + aggregate_id + timestamp). Handlers check for duplicate processing to prevent double GL entries.

decisions.decision: JSON serialization for event payloads in outbox
decisions.rationale: Jackson JSON in the payload column. Enables inspection, debugging, manual replay. Event records are simple data classes that serialize cleanly.

decisions.decision: Polling-based outbox processor (5s interval) for MVP
decisions.rationale: Simple @Scheduled every 5 seconds. PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY is an optimization for later if latency matters. Keep it simple.

constraints: Outbox processor must be idempotent (safe to restart)
JSON serialization must handle UUID, BigDecimal, Instant, LocalDate, String, enums
Dead letter table needs unique constraint on outbox_id
Liquibase migrations for event_outbox and event_dead_letter tables
TransitionGuard registered for all 4 status enums before removing ad-hoc checks
All new entities extend BaseEntity (id, timestamps, version)
openQuestions: Should outbox processor use LISTEN/NOTIFY instead of polling for lower latency? (Recommendation: defer, polling is sufficient)
Should we add admin endpoint for dead letter inspection/retry? (Recommendation: defer to follow-up task)
Should TransitionGuard support transition callbacks/actions? (Recommendation: no, keep guards pure)
